title: senecio adscendens
intro: Senecio adscendens, also known as Jacob's ladder, is a perennial plant native to South Africa, characterized by its delicate, fern-like foliage and clusters of small, blue or white flowers. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Senecio adscendens include treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from fever and pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
